name: Natural Park of the Serra de Mariola — Rodeo from Biar
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra de Mariola — Rodeo desde Biar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Biar are set within the Vinalopó Valley, at the foothills of the Sierra de Mariola. The region features mountainous terrain with peaks exceeding 1,000 meters, extensive pine forests, and dramatic rock formations. Natural springs and historic aqueducts are also characteristic of the landscape, providing varied scenery for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Biar The most popular road cycling route is Natural Park of the Serra de Mariola — Rodeo from Biar, a 45.3 miles (72.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging climbs through the protected mountain range. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Biar (810 m) – Onil Recreation Center loop from Biar, a moderate 27.1 miles (43.6 km) path. This loop includes a significant mountain pass and varied terrain around the recreation center. Local road cyclists also love the Biar – Pou Clar Natural Area loop from Biar, a 56.8 miles (91.4 km) trail leading through natural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Biar is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and natural springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Biar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Biar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Biar?
A: Biar offers a diverse network of nearly 20 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's an option for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Biar?
A: Road cycling in Biar features varied terrain, from the mountainous landscapes of the Sierra de Mariola with peaks exceeding 1,000 meters to serene pine forests and valleys. You'll also find routes utilizing former railway lines, offering traffic-free sections like the Vía Verde del Xixarra, and dramatic rock formations.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Biar?
A: May is a particularly popular month for cyclists to visit Biar, thanks to favorable weather conditions. The region generally offers good cycling weather for much of the spring and autumn.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Biar offers routes suitable for all abilities. There are at least two easy-grade road cycling routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Biar?
A: The road cycling routes around Biar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 600 road cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890895702/natural-park-of-the-serra-de-mariola-rodeo-from-biar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Serra de Mariola — Rodeo from Biar&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, with over 990 meters of ascent over 72.9 km, taking you through the protected mountain range.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in Biar?
A: Yes, Biar provides options for longer rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24536725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar – Pou Clar Natural Area loop from Biar&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 91.4 km trail that can be completed in around 4 hours, leading through beautiful natural areas.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Biar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38227495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Biar (810 m) – Onil Recreation Center loop from Biar&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 43.6 km path that includes a significant mountain pass.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes in Biar?
A: Cycling routes in Biar often traverse the stunning Serra de Mariola Natural Park, characterized by extensive pine forests, Mediterranean woodlands, and dramatic rock formations. You might also pass by natural springs, such as the source of the Vinalopó River, and even waterfalls.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Biar is rich in history. You can explore landmarks such as the imposing 12th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1090694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, or the 15th-century Nuestra Señora de la Asunción Parish Church. The region also features a 1490 medieval aqueduct and the ancient Iberian Village of Cabeçó de Mariola.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that include mountain passes?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Biar means many routes feature challenging ascents and descents over mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Biar (810 m) – Canalís d'Onil Pass loop from Biar&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, incorporating two significant passes for a rewarding ride.

Q: What viewpoints or scenic spots can I find on the routes?
A: The region offers numerous scenic spots. From the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1090694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar Castle&lt;/a&gt;, you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views of olive groves and the distant Sierra de Mariola. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6668923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Reconco from Alt de la Replana&lt;/a&gt;, providing stunning vistas of the surrounding peaks.
tips: It is one of the largest and most picturesque parks in the interior of the Valencian Community and was declared a natural park in 2002. It is a place that offers a wide variety of activities in nature, as well as countless paths, paths and paths with different surfaces and difficulties, which make it a paradise for all lovers of outdoor activities.
